conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules, and then I'll ask you some questions about your circumstances.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit. To start, I need to understand your situation:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 9 years old. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Leeds.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards his upkeep - for example on food, clothes or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives at home with me. He's been with me since he was born.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for that information. Just a few more questions:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in local authority care for more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex is happy and healthy at home with me. He's not in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- Alex lives with you, which means you're responsible for him ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓
- Alex is not in hospital or care ✓

You meet all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You can proceed with making a claim to receive this benefit.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK (Leeds) and Alex lives at home with them, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital for more than 12 weeks or in local authority care for more than 8 weeks. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and they live in the UK. A person is usually responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 9-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Alex is not in hospital or care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

The claimant lives in the UK (Leeds) and Alex lives at home with them, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital for more than 12 weeks or in local authority care for more than 8 weeks. According to the eligibility rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and they live in the UK. A person is usually responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 9-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex as they live together, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Alex is not in hospital or care. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
